cards.back: A method for unconstrained optimization that moves in the direction of the negative gradient at each step to minimize the objective function.
A type of gradient descent method that also uses a temperature parameter to adjust the step size and control the convergence rate.
A widely used optimization algorithm for unconstrained problems that moves in the direction of the negative gradient at each step to minimize the objective function.
A variant of gradient descent that uses a small batch of samples to compute the gradient at each step, improving the convergence rate and stability.
A variant of gradient descent that uses a single sample to compute the gradient at each step, which is more computationally efficient but may converge more slowly.
